Teen Digital Resources Teen Digital Resources Pathfinders Teen Volunteer Opportunities Teen Volunteer Opportunities Teen Advisory Board Parent/Teacher Resources Parent/Teacher Resources Teacher Services Request (Grades 6-12) Search Borrowing Policies Home » About Us » Mission and Policies » Borrowing Policies » Borrowing Policies Minuteman Library Network The Medford Public Library is a member of the Minuteman Library Network. Card holders at the Medford Library may use their cards at any MLN library and vice versa. Loan periods, fine rates, and other policies may differ at each library. A list of the MLN member libraries is available at the Information Desk or here . To access MLN online, go to https://www.minlib.net/ . Registering for a Library Card Library cards are issued to Massachusetts residents who present a piece of identification with their name and current address. Children under age 18 may obtain a card if accompanied by a parent or guardian. 7th grade and older must be accompanied by a parent or guardian, or present signed permission in writing and a piece of identification with name and address. Cards issued in Medford may be used at any library in the Minuteman Library Network. Identification may include a valid driver’s license, imprinted bank check, piece of delivered non-personal mail such as a bill, bank statement, or magazine with a mailing label. Out-of-state residents who work in Massachusetts, are students in Massachusetts, own property in Massachusetts, or are living in temporarily in Massachusetts (for a minimum of 8 weeks), may register for a card. Work ID that includes a current mailing address, or a student ID that includes a current mailing address, or a piece of delivered non-personal mail is required for verification of a Massachusetts mailing address. Patrons may apply for a temporary card online by going to https://www.minlib.net/ecard and providing a valid email address. A card number will be emailed to that address which can be used to borrow materials from the digital catalog and place holds in the Minuteman catalog. A temporary library card typically expires after six months so a patron must physically visit the Library and provide a photo ID with current address in order to receive a permanent card. Checking Out If your card is lost, you may use a photo ID instead or get a new card and number issued at no cost. Confidentiality of Records It is the policy of the Medford Public Library, as well as the Minuteman Library Network, to guard the confidentiality of all library users. The computerized system maintains no trace in the patron’s record of items that have been borrowed and returned unless an overdue charge is still outstanding. Once a fine is paid, all record of the transaction is eliminated. Using a library card number and PIN, patrons can set up a “Reading History” account which will keep track of all items borrowed. This service is only accessible to the patron in the My Account feature of the website. Interlibrary Loan and Reserves Reciprocal borrowing is provided for MLN card holders through the Minuteman Library Network. Requests may be placed in person, by phone, on online. To place a request online, you must obtain a password for your account (visit https://www.minlib.net/minlib.net or see “Online Accounts” below). Card holders will be notified by telephone or e-mail when requested items are available. Materials not available in the MLN may be obtained through other networks. Loan Periods Loan Periods at the Medford Public Library are: Books: 3 weeks Audiobooks, CD’s, & Magazines: 3 weeks DVDs: 2 weeks Museum Passes: 1 day Books from other libraries may have different loan periods. The Medford Public Library assumes no responsibility for damage to equipment. Lost and Damaged Materials Patrons will be charged for lost or damaged items according to the list price of the item or the generic price entered into the bibliographic record. Patrons may also replace a lost or damaged item with an identical item, newly purchased. If an item is lost and paid for, the patron must provide either a receipt or cancelled check to receive a refund. Refunds are issued through City Hall and can take time to process. If you are paying for a lost item, be sure to request a receipt. Materials borrowed from other MLN libraries may be paid for at Medford by check or money order only. If you want to replace an item from another library, you must contact that library directly to make sure they will accept a replacement. Notices If you have an e-mail address associated with your account, you will receive all notices and communication by e-mail. This includes notification when holds become available, 2-day reminders that books are going to be due, and notifications about bills and fines. You may also opt into text alerts by texting “SIGNUP” to (833) 201-1813. Online Accounts MLN card holders may sign-up for a password in order to access their borrowing records online and view and renew materials they’ve checked out, view fines and overdue items, request downloadable materials, and view, place or cancel requests. Patrons may request that a password of their choosing be added to their account at the Circulation, Information, or Reference Desks. Passwords must be between 3 and 8 characters and can be numbers and/or letters. Online accounts can be accessed in your browser or with the MLN app, which also includes a digital library card, which can be shown on a mobile device in place of your physical card. Overdue Fines The library encourages the prompt return of materials borrowed by patrons but, with the exception of museum passes, no overdue fees will be charged for late returns. An item that has been out 28 days beyond its due date will be charged a replacement cost which can be paid instead of returning the item. Patrons may continue to check out materials as long as their fee amount is under $100.00. Fines can be paid by cash or check at any MLN library and by debit/credit card online in the patron’s online account. Fine rates are as follows: Charges for late Museum Passes are $10.00 per day, $30.00 maximum per pass. Out of Network (ILL) fees may be incurred if the loading library insists. Renewals All Medford materials may be renewed two times – in person, by telephone, or online. An item cannot be renewed if it is reserved for another patron. Items from other MLN libraries may be renewed twice at Medford, but please be aware that renewal limits and policies are different at each MLN library . Items that are not on hold will automatically renew on their due date. However, new items and items with pending holds will not. Out of network ILL books cannot be renewed. Returns Materials may be returned at the Circulation Desk during the Library’s regular hours of operation. Book Drops are available at each entrance. There is a fine of $5.00 for Museum Passes returned in the book drop. Please bring Passes into the library or to the Information Desk for return. Materials from other MLN libraries may be returned to Medford, subject to Medford’s fine rates. Materials from Non-MLN libraries will be accepted at Medford, but will not be checked in until they reach the home library. pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "87 Mount Vernon Street, Boston, MA 02108","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-by-foot_50_md.jpg","telephone": "617-367-2345","name": "Boston by Foot ","url": "","description": "Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston&apos;s rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes."} Boston by Foot 87 Mount Vernon Street, Boston, MA 02108617-367-2345http://www.bostonbyfoot.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston by Foot Boston By Foot has presented walking tours of Boston since 1976. Dedicated to promoting public awareness and appreciation of Boston's rich history and architectural heritage, their guided tours and programs are conducted by a dedicated corps of over 200 volunteer tour guides. Check their website for a list of available tours, most of which take 90 minutes.
Categories: Children, Family, History, Tours
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Free admission to most of Boston By Foot’s Walking Tours (with the exception of Architecture tours, private tours, "Boston with a BOO!" and "Footloose on the Freedom Trail"). 
Available seasonally from April- October.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 (adults or children) at half price per person.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits Use this code for 50% off of up to 4 tickets to Spectacle or Georges Island.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-concord-museum_10_md.png","telephone": "978-369-9763","name": "Concord Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson&apos;s study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on. "} Concord Museum 200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742978-369-9763http://www.concordmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Concord Museum Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
Categories: Family, History
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Each pass admits up to 4 people at $5 off per person.
Not to be combined with other discounts; not valid for member pricing.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
Discovery Museum Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
Categories: Children, Family, Science
Pass Benefits  This pass admits 3 visitors at 1/2 price admission per person; any additional visitors must pay the regular admission price.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "67 Shore Road, Winchester, MA 01890","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-griffin-museum-of-photography_63_md.jpg","telephone": "781-729-1158","name": "Griffin Museum of Photography","url": "","description": "Opened in 1992, the Griffin Museum of Photography presents exhibitions, programs and lectures that support their mission of encouraging a broader understanding and appreciation of the art of photography. Three galleries are dedicated to changing exhibitions of photography by well-known, emerging, and student photographers. (Three additional satellite galleries can be found in various locations in the Boston area.)"} Griffin Museum of Photography 67 Shore Road, Winchester, MA 01890781-729-1158http://www.griffinmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Griffin Museum of Photography Opened in 1992, the Griffin Museum of Photography presents exhibitions, programs and lectures that support their mission of encouraging a broader understanding and appreciation of the art of photography. Three galleries are dedicated to changing exhibitions of photography by well-known, emerging, and student photographers. (Three additional satellite galleries can be found in various locations in the Boston area.)
Categories: Art, Family
